content:
# 2 Peter Chapter 2 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (Aleph, 01), page 270 | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/2PE_002_page_001.jpg

1. But there came also false prophets among the people, as also among you there will be false teachers, who will bring in alongside destructive heresies, even denying the Master who bought them, bringing upon themselves swift destruction.

2. And many will follow the shameless deeds of them, through whom the way of the truth will be blasphemed.

3. And in covetousness, with fabricated words, they will exploit you — for whom the judgment from long ago is not idle, and the destruction of them does not slumber.

4. For if the God did not spare angels having sinned, but having cast [them] into Tartaros, delivered [them] to chains of darkness, being kept for judgment;

5. and [he] did not spare [the] ancient world, but [the] eighth [one], Noah, a herald of righteousness, he guarded, having brought a flood upon [the] world of [the] ungodly;

6. and [the] cities of Sodom and Gomorrah, having reduced to ashes, he condemned with an overthrow, having set [them as] an example to those about to be ungodly;

7. and righteous Lot, being oppressed by the conduct of the lawless [ones] in shameless deeds, he rescued —

8. for by sight and by hearing, the righteous [one] dwelling among them, day after day, [his] righteous soul was being tormented by [their] lawless works —

9. [the] Lord knows [how] to rescue [the] godly out of trial, and to keep [the] unrighteous for [the] day of judgment, being punished,

10. and especially the [ones] going after [the] flesh in lust of defilement and despising lordship. Daring [ones], self-willed [ones], they do not tremble blaspheming glories,

11. whereas angels, being greater in strength and power, do not bring against them a blasphemous judgment before [the] Lord.

12. But these, as irrational animals born naturally for capture and destruction, blaspheming in things which they are ignorant of, in the destruction of them they will also be destroyed,

13. being wronged [as] wages of unrighteousness. [They are ones] considering the revel in [the] daytime a pleasure; spots and blemishes, reveling in the deceits of them while feasting together with you,

14. having eyes full of an adulteress and unceasing from sin, enticing unstable souls, having a heart exercised in covetousness — children of a curse!

15. Having left [the] straight way, they went astray, having followed the way of Balaam [son] of Bosor, who loved [the] wages of unrighteousness,

16. but had a rebuke of [his] own transgression: a voiceless beast of burden, having spoken in a human voice, restrained the madness of the prophet.

17. These are waterless springs and mists driven by a storm, for whom the gloom of the darkness has been kept.

18. For speaking swollen [words] of vanity, they entice in lusts of [the] flesh, by shameless deeds, the [ones] barely escaping from the [ones] living in error,

19. promising them freedom, while they themselves are slaves of the corruption; for by what a person has been overcome, by this he has been enslaved.

20. For if, having escaped the defilements of the world by [the] full knowledge of the Lord and Savior Yeshua Christos, and having been entangled in these again, they are overcome, the last [things] have become worse for them than the first.

21. For it were better for them not to have known the way of the righteousness, than having known [it], to turn back from the holy commandment having been delivered to them.

22. It has happened to them [according to] the [word] of the true proverb: "[A] dog having returned to [its] own vomit," and, "[A] sow having been washed, to [her] rolling in [the] mud."

---

## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **2:1 — "destructive heresies" (αιρεσεις απωλειας):** Sinaiticus reads ΑΙΡΕCΕΙC ΑΠΩΛΕΙΑC. The literal sense is "heresies of destruction," rendered here as "destructive heresies" for clarity while preserving the genitive relationship.

2. **2:2 — "shameless deeds" (ασελγειαις):** Sinaiticus reads ΑCΕΛΓΕΙΑΙC, literally "acts of licentiousness" or "shameless deeds." Some manuscripts read "destructive ways" (απωλειαις) instead. Sinaiticus supports the reading ΑCΕΛΓΕΙΑΙC.

3. **2:4 — "chains of darkness" (σειραις ζοφου):** Sinaiticus reads CΕΙΡΑΙC ΖΟΦΟΥ ("chains of darkness"). Some manuscripts read CΙΡΟΙC ("pits") instead of CΕΙΡΑΙC ("chains"). The Sinaiticus reading is translated here.

4. **2:5 — "the eighth, Noah":** The Greek ΟΓΔΟΟΝ ΝΩΕΑ literally reads "eighth, Noah," meaning Noah was the eighth person preserved. This is an idiomatic way of saying Noah plus seven others were saved.

5. **2:6 — "Sodom and Gomorrah":** Sinaiticus spells Gomorrah as ΓΟΜΟΡΡΑC. The city names are transliterated from the Greek forms.

6. **2:11 — "before the Lord" (παρα κυριω):** Sinaiticus reads ΠΑΡΑ ΚΥΡΙΩ ("before [the] Lord"). Some manuscripts read ΠΑΡΑ ΚΥΡΙΟΥ ("from [the] Lord"). The Sinaiticus dative reading is followed here.

7. **2:13 — "being wronged as wages" (αδικουμενοι μισθον):** Sinaiticus reads ΑΔΙΚΟΥΜΕΝΟΙ ("being wronged") rather than ΚΟΜΙΟΥΜΕΝΟΙ ("receiving") found in some manuscripts. This is a distinctive Sinaiticus reading, creating a wordplay: they who do unrighteousness (αδικια) are themselves wronged (αδικουμενοι).

8. **2:13 — "deceits" (απαταις):** Sinaiticus reads ΑΠΑΤΑΙC ("deceits") where some manuscripts read ΑΓΑΠΑΙC ("love-feasts"). The Sinaiticus reading is followed.

9. **2:15 — "Bosor" (Βοσορ):** Sinaiticus reads ΒΟCΟΡ rather than the Old Testament form "Beor." This variant spelling is preserved in the translation.

10. **2:17 — "waterless springs":** Sinaiticus reads ΠΗΓΑΙ ΑΝΥΔΡΟΙ ("waterless springs"). The metaphor describes false teachers who promise refreshment but deliver nothing.

11. **2:18 — "barely" (ολιγως):** Sinaiticus reads ΟΛΙΓΩC ("barely/slightly"). Some manuscripts read ΟΝΤΩC ("truly/really"). The Sinaiticus reading "barely escaping" is followed here.

12. **2:20 — "full knowledge" (επιγνωσει):** The compound ΕΠΙΓΝΩCΕΙ (epi + gnosis) denotes thorough, experiential knowledge, stronger than simple ΓΝΩCΙC. Rendered "full knowledge" to preserve this distinction.

13. **2:22 — Proverb citation:** The first part alludes to Proverbs 26:11 (LXX). The second part ("a sow having been washed...") is not from the Hebrew Bible and may derive from an extra-biblical proverb or oral tradition.
